items.title: Scale features before eps tuning
items.avoid: Grid-searching eps on raw mixed-unit columns.
items.prefer: StandardScaler in a sklearn Pipeline; run k-distance plots and DBSCAN on scaled X only.
items.why: eps is one radius applied equally to all dimensions — comparable scales make the radius semantically meaningful.

items.title: Use k-distance elbow for initial eps
items.avoid: Guessing eps = 0.5 because a blog post used it on a different dataset.
items.prefer: Plot sorted (min_samples−1)-th neighbor distances on scaled data; choose eps at the knee; confirm with maps or metrics.
items.why: The knee reflects intrinsic spacing of your point cloud — the main data-driven starting point for eps.
items.example: min_samples=5 → plot 4-th NN distance sorted; eps ≈ value at visible elbow.

items.title: Set min_samples relative to dimensionality
items.avoid: Always using min_samples=2 regardless of feature count.
items.prefer: Start with min_samples ≥ d + 1 (or 2·d for noisy data); adjust if noise fraction or cluster count is unreasonable.
items.why: Higher dimensions need more neighbors to declare a region truly dense rather than a random sparse coincidence.
items.example: d=10 features → try min_samples=11 or 20 before accepting 80% noise labels.

items.title: Validate outputs beyond cluster count
items.avoid: Accepting the first run because n_clusters > 0.
items.prefer: Check noise percentage, cluster size distribution, stability under subsampling, and Cluster Evaluation metrics on assigned points.
items.why: A single giant cluster or all-noise partition can still have a positive cluster count — domain and metrics catch failure modes.
items.example: Report: 3 clusters, 12% noise, silhouette=0.41 on non-noise points, stable across 5 seeds.

items.title: Match algorithm to density structure
items.avoid: Using DBSCAN when clusters are known spherical and equal-sized with no outliers.
items.prefer: Use k-means for simple partitions; reserve DBSCAN for irregular shapes, unknown k, or explicit noise handling.
items.why: Algorithm choice should follow geometry — DBSCAN's strengths are density connectivity and noise labels, not speed on uniform blobs.
items.example: Three round iris species blobs on scaled features → k-means may suffice; GPS event arcs → DBSCAN.
